title: Sweet Sticky Filipino Banana Pudding
description: A traditional Filipino dessert featuring bananas, sweetened condensed milk, and a caramel-like lacquer glaze, perfect for satisfying sweet cravings and soothing hangovers.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the sweetened condensed milk, evaporated milk, and granulated sugar, and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar has dissolved.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and water until smooth, then add the mixture to the saucepan and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture has thickened.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the vanilla extract and unsalted butter until the butter has melted.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and salt until well combined.
Add the sliced bananas to the bowl with the eggs and toss to coat.
Add the cooked milk mixture to the bowl with the bananas and eggs, and stir until the bananas are well coated.
Pour the mixture into a 9x13 inch baking dish and smooth the top.
Bake the pudding in a preheated oven at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the pudding is set.
Remove the pudding from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Once the pudding has cooled, refrigerate it for at least 2 hours, or until chilled.
